# RFC-0001: Specification Overview And Authority Map

> **Version:** 0.64.1 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** impl

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0001:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0001c-summary"></a>

InferLab runs reproducible LLM inference experiments: a versioned workspace fixes the shareable baseline, stacks own framework realizations, independently launchable servers own serving intent and cases, recipes compose one server with one workload suite, local bindings supply concrete machine placement, and every execution writes durable file-first evidence. This document is the specification entry point and maps each contract area to its owning RFC.

**Authority map:**

- RFC-0002 — workspace, stack, and local-placement authority: revision and source identity, local bindings and dirty-workspace policy, Pixi lifecycle, stack realization checks, ad-hoc execution, and runtime caches.
- RFC-0003 — server, recipe, and runtime execution: server and case composition, one resolved authority, long-running and closed-loop workflows, canonical topology and per-role parallelism, and routing ownership.
- RFC-0004 — measurements and toolchains: Eval and Bench semantics, InferLab-owned measurement toolchains, and workload profiling.
- RFC-0005 — evidence: immutable resolved execution records, runtime evidence, and operator journal.
- RFC-0006 — adapter protocol version 6 and independently distributed integration packages.
- RFC-0007 — stack-based runtime image production, validation, and container launch facts.
- RFC-0008 — agent plugin distribution.
- RFC-0009 — runtime time-bound semantics: operation budgets, nested attempt bounds, measurement-case deadlines, deliberately unbounded operations, and cleanup grace.
- RFC-0010 — view-only workspace TUI: navigation across declared definitions, current CLI operations, records, and bounded live observations.
- RFC-0011 — public product and documentation website: content authority, current public-snapshot presentation, static validation, and GitHub Pages delivery.
- This document — cross-cutting operator progress and output streams (RFC-0001:C-OPERATOR-PROGRESS), error codes (RFC-0001:C-ERROR-CODES), and license-notice retention (RFC-0001:C-LICENSE-RETENTION).

**Scope:** The specification covers externally observable configuration authority, command semantics, protocol boundaries, and evidence. It does not prescribe crate layout, private implementation types, arbitrary workflow graphs, registry publication, or compatibility with configuration and record schemas predating this contract.

**Conformance language:** MUST, MUST NOT, SHOULD, and MAY are normative keywords with their customary specification meaning across every RFC in this set.

**Rationale:** The contract fixes the operator capabilities proved necessary — fixed-workspace installation, direct and closed-loop server lifecycle, heterogeneous multi-role placement, measurement, images, profiling, and durable evidence — while keeping production-service supervision, provider indirection, universal workflow planning, and generic measurement dependencies out of the serving stack. Topic RFCs own and version their areas independently.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

### [RFC-0001:C-ERROR-CODES] Error Code Registry (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-error-codes"></a>

InferLab reports every runtime failure under exactly one error code. When a command fails, the CLI MUST exit nonzero and MUST write one diagnostic line to stderr of the form `error[<code>]: <message>`. Codes are the machine-branchable contract; message text is guidance and MAY change between releases. Command-line usage errors (argument parsing) are reported by the argument parser and are outside this registry.

**Registry.** The leading digit groups codes by failing subsystem.

| Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| E1001 | Workspace discovery failed: no workspace manifest found from the start directory upward. |
| E1002 | A required file could not be read. |
| E1003 | Configuration could not be parsed or serialized. |
| E1004 | Configuration is invalid: well-formed input that violates the contract. |
| E1005 | A server override value is invalid. |
| E1006 | A git operation failed. |
| E1007 | The committed Pixi environment lifecycle failed: realization, launch, restoration, or an unusable environment. |
| E1008 | The measurement toolchain failed: unsupported platform, installation, verification, launch, or a missing toolchain. |
| E2001 | A framework integration failed: launch, transport, timeout, protocol violation, or rejection. |
| E3001 | Placement failed: a machine binding provides fewer devices than the server requires. |
| E4001 | A closed-loop run (recipe, Bench, or image build) failed after finalizing its record; the message names the record id. |
| E4002 | A server lifecycle operation, the built-in proxy, or profiling failed. |
| E4003 | An image build or image selection was rejected. |
| E5001 | Server record access, decoding, or encoding failed. |
| E5002 | A workspace operation observation could not be encoded, published, updated, or removed. |
| E6001 | A scratchpad journal operation failed. |
| E7001 | An agent plugin operation failed. |
| E8001 | An ad-hoc environment execution was rejected or could not launch the operator command. |
| E9001 | Command output could not be written or encoded. |

**Stability.** The registry is append-only. A published code MUST keep its meaning across releases and MUST NOT be reused for a different meaning. A new failure condition MAY join an existing code only when it matches that code's stated meaning; otherwise a new code MUST be added. Consumers MAY branch on codes and MUST NOT need to parse message text.

*Since: v0.58.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-LICENSE-RETENTION] License Notice Retention (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-license-retention"></a>

Every distributed InferLab artifact MUST retain the project's license notice text; a license identifier or SPDX expression alone does not satisfy retention.

Per artifact form:

- Packages — Python wheels and sdists, and published crates — MUST contain the license file, byte-identical to the repository text, and a build or packaging gate MUST fail when a package copy drifts from that text or a built artifact lacks it.
- The agent plugin package MUST pack the license file, and packaging MUST fail when it is absent.
- The binary MUST embed the notice and print it with `inferlab license`, so a bare binary download retains it with no companion file.
- Each release MUST publish the license as a first-class asset for human discovery.

**Rationale:** The license's own condition attaches to copies of the software, so retention holds by construction in every artifact form rather than by anyone remembering to bundle a file: index installs, release downloads, plugin installs, and bare binary copies each carry the notice through the artifact itself.

*Since: v0.59.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-OPERATOR-PROGRESS] Operator Progress And Output Streams (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-operator-progress"></a>

InferLab MUST preserve stdout as the command result stream. Progress MUST NOT appear on stdout for a command that returns a machine-readable or human-readable InferLab result.

Progress governed by this clause MUST be emitted as complete newline-terminated text lines on stderr. Its meaning MUST NOT depend on cursor control, animation, color, or terminal detection. Progress wording beyond the facts required by this clause is operator guidance and MAY change between releases; callers MUST use final command output and [RFC-0001:C-ERROR-CODES](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-error-codes), not progress text, for machine branching.

`workspace lock`, `toolchain install`, non-dry-run `serve start`, `serve stop`, non-dry-run `recipe run`, non-dry-run `bench`, and non-dry-run `image build` MUST report their first phase before waiting on a lock or starting their first potentially blocking external operation. Afterward they MUST report each applicable phase when it begins.

`stack status`, `serve status`, `serve logs`, `scratchpad note`, `scratchpad show`, and the dry-run forms of `serve start`, `recipe run`, `bench`, and `image build` MAY remain quiet when they complete within ten seconds. If one of those commands has not completed within ten seconds, it MUST report its current phase no later than the ten-second point and MUST report each subsequent phase when it begins.

A reported phase that remains active for ten seconds MUST emit a heartbeat no later than ten seconds after that phase was first reported. It MUST continue to emit heartbeats no more than thirty seconds apart until the phase ends. Each heartbeat MUST identify the command, the current phase, and the elapsed duration of that phase.

When a phase processes a bounded collection, progress MUST identify the current item and its ordinal position and total when those facts are known. A readiness heartbeat MUST include the most recent readiness failure when one exists. A lock-wait heartbeat MUST identify the contended lock.

The governed commands MUST expose these operator phases when they apply:

- `workspace lock`: base-lock production, staged base installation, authoritative full-lock production, and restoration after failure or interruption;
- `toolchain install`: installation-state inspection, writer-lock waiting, incomplete-installation replacement, Pixi installation, Eval verification, and Bench verification;
- `serve start`: resolution, local and remote preflight, process launch, and readiness for each resolved process;
- `serve stop`: profiler finalization, process termination, container removal, and log synchronization for each resolved process;
- `recipe run`: resolution, server startup, each Eval, each Bench case or adaptive probe, profiler finalization when selected, and server cleanup;
- `bench`: resolution and admission, each Bench case or adaptive probe, and profiler finalization when selected;
- `image build`: resolution plus the record, package-build, assembly, inspection, export, and validation phases required by [RFC-0007:C-IMAGE-BUILD](../rfc/RFC-0007.md#rfc-0007c-image-build);
- `scratchpad note` and `scratchpad show`: journal-lock waiting.

When a command creates a durable record, the next progress line after creation MUST identify the record identifier and record directory, and it MUST be emitted before any subsequent potentially blocking operation. When a durable log path exists before a potentially blocking external operation starts, that operation's phase report MUST include the log path.

Progress is ephemeral operator guidance. It MUST NOT replace authoritative execution records, command evidence, result artifacts, or failure diagnostics, and it MUST NOT be reconstructed into those artifacts as a second authority.

`inferlab run` MUST NOT emit wrapper progress after the operator command starts, because that command owns its stdout and stderr under [RFC-0002:C-ADHOC-EXECUTION](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-adhoc-execution). Fast local-only commands for workspace display and license output have no progress requirement. `agent install`, `agent update`, `agent uninstall`, and `agent doctor` have no phase or heartbeat requirement under this clause; their result and error behavior remains governed by [RFC-0008:C-AGENT-PLUGIN](../rfc/RFC-0008.md#rfc-0008c-agent-plugin) and [RFC-0001:C-ERROR-CODES](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-error-codes).

The terminal InferLab result and the error-code behavior of [RFC-0001:C-ERROR-CODES](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-error-codes) remain unchanged. A progress line MUST NOT be formatted as a terminal `error[<code>]: <message>` diagnostic.

**Rationale:** Long InferLab workflows intentionally capture protocol output and preserve external-tool and experiment output in durable logs, while stdout carries one final report. Without a separate progress contract, a correctly advancing installation, readiness wait, measurement matrix, or cleanup is indistinguishable from a stalled command. Semantic phases, bounded elapsed-time heartbeats, and early record and log discovery make the operation observable without corrupting stdout, duplicating record authority, exposing protocol streams, or inventing unreliable percentages and completion estimates.

*Since: v0.63.0*
